utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all 5 appear. Now check if each is fully specified with required details: (1) health_coverage: requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Provided: physical with plan and cost, mental with plan and cost — fully specified. (2) property_coverage: requires home/contents insurance with costs. Provided: a plan name and a monthly cost — meets the requirement (home/contents insurance with costs). (3) travel_coverage: requires travel insurance with costs. Provided: plan and cost — specified. (4) additional_services: requires any enhancement services with costs. Provided: 'N/A (covered within chosen plans)'. While it indicates none, it does not list costs; however, 'N/A' is acceptable as no additional services are selected, so it's still specified. (5) total_budget: requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Provided: '€340/month' only; no annual breakdown. Therefore, not fully specified according to the requirement for a complete monthly/annual breakdown.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three specific items: HealthFirst Standard Plan for physical health, HealthFirst Standard Plan for mental health, HomeFirst Standard Coverage for property, and PrimeTravel Premium Plan for travel. I located each of these in the ground-truth ratings and extracted their exact ratings: HealthFirst Standard Plan (physical health) is rated 7; HealthFirst Standard Plan (mental health) is rated 6; HomeFirst Standard Coverage is rated 7; PrimeTravel Premium Plan is rated 10.
